question: Reviewer surface separation: a separate bench review serve verb and process with import-contract isolation from harness.serve/harness.status, or role-gated routes on a single server?
author: claude/design

question: Reviewer identity: bound once at launch through resolve_actor (bench review serve --reviewer), or supplied per capture request?
author: claude/design

question: Capture hotkeys: 1/2/T/C mapped to our winner vocabulary (1|2|TIE|CANT_JUDGE) with enter-advance, or the LangSmith pairwise A/B/E convention?
author: claude/design

question: Packet transport: serve the built packet bytes verbatim (what a CLI reviewer would open), or re-render packets live per request?
author: claude/design

rationale: Recommended option accepted via 2026-07-04 session directive ('Begin implementing in the sequence you recommend'). Isolation must be structural: its own process, its own route table, and an import contract forbidding harness.serve/harness.status — a role flag on one server would be a discipline, not a property.
author: jyang

rationale: Recommended option accepted via 2026-07-04 session directive. One reviewer per process through resolve_actor — refused loudly, never 'unknown' — matching every other ledgering surface; per-request identity would make attribution a client claim.
author: jyang

rationale: Recommended option accepted via 2026-07-04 session directive. 1/2/T/C maps to the ledgered winner vocabulary (1|2|TIE|CANT_JUDGE) — keys that say what they record beat a borrowed convention that needs translation.
author: jyang

rationale: Recommended option accepted via 2026-07-04 session directive. The reviewer sees exactly the packet bytes a CLI reviewer would open (idempotent build, ledgered response order), re-scanned against identity canaries before serving; a live re-render would be a second packet pipeline to keep blind.
author: jyang
